title: ProtePilot — Developability Decision Orchestrator
tagline: Routes a candidate sequence through specialized predictors — aggregation, stability, immunogenicity, manufacturability, process economics — and emits an integrated go/no-go verdict with per-dimension confidence scores.
key_facts: Covers 10 molecule formats from canonical mAbs to bispecifics, nanobodies, ADCs, and engineered scaffolds.
138 quality gate checks are reported as all passing.
Benchmarks include 246 clinical-stage antibodies across 13 experimental assays.
PROPHET-Ab results report 8/13 significant correlations with 13/13 correct direction.
IEDB validation reports ROC AUC = 0.699 on 5,000 human MHC-II T-cell epitopes.

title: ab-benchmark
tagline: This package treats TAP, DI, CamSol, and PROPHET-Ab as comparable baselines under a common evaluation harness — grouped-repeated CV clustered on V-gene family + CDR-H3 length + 50% CDR-H3 identity, with bootstrap 95% CIs on every metric.
key_facts: Phase 0 audit reports 1,243 harmonized records covering 1,106 unique antibodies.
7 baseline families: TAP, Developability Index, CamSol-intrinsic, BioPhi/OASis, DynaMine, CABS-flex, and PROPHET-Ab ridge.
Grouped-repeated CV clustered on V-gene family + CDR-H3 length + 50% CDR-H3 identity.
Every metric framed with bootstrap 95% confidence intervals.
Test suite reports 99/99 passing at 71% coverage. Released as v0.1.0.

title: struct-devpred
tagline: On the harmonized Jain-2017 clinical-stage antibody panel, do structure-informed sequence descriptors (TAP, Developability Index, CamSol) — or PLM embeddings, or a hybrid — beat simple physicochemical/composition baselines for predicting biophysical endpoints under leakage-resistant cross-validation?
key_facts: Best hic_rt model reaches Spearman rho +0.41 with 95% CI [+0.26, +0.54].
hybrid_seq_struct ranks first with mean rho +0.333 across six endpoints.
Computes 7 descriptor families; trains 3 model families per endpoint under 5-fold GroupKFold.
All cells report Spearman rho with bootstrap 95% CI.
Inputs include 137 x 960 ESM-2 t12 embeddings from ProtePilot.

title: bo-protein-dms
tagline: On the GB1 4-position DMS landscape (Wu et al. 2016), do discrete, sequence-aware Bayesian-optimization strategies beat random search for a 96-query protein-engineering campaign — under >= 20 seeds with bootstrap 95 % confidence intervals?
key_facts: On a 160,000-variant GB1 landscape, active BO reaches final best fitness 5.99 vs 2.57 for random at 96-query budget.
turbo_lite reaches 90% of maximum in 31 rounds.
Four strategies benchmarked: random, top_predicted_ridge, gp_hamming, and turbo_lite.
All results under >= 20 seeds with bootstrap 95% confidence intervals.
Released as v0.1.1 on GitHub.

title: constrained-cdr-gen
tagline: Does property-guided Gibbs sampling over CDR positions produce more developable candidate antibodies than unguided masked-LM sampling or random AA substitution — under evaluation by an independent developability oracle that the sampler never saw?
key_facts: Guided sampling reaches pass rate 0.850 with 95% CI [0.76, 0.93] vs 0.650 unguided and 0.438 random.
Guided ESM perplexity is 9.12 vs 13.07 for unguided sampling.
Independent oracle uses 4 checks: TAP risk flags, Developability Index, CamSol-intrinsic, and humanness proxy.
Generates 200 candidate CDR-H3 variants on Trastuzumab.
Diversity among passing designs maintained at >= 0.8x unguided.

title: oglycan-optimizer
tagline: Given a glycoprotein's site catalog, it tunes 6 instrument and workflow parameters — ETD supplemental activation, MS2 resolution, HCD collision energy, enzyme prep, LC gradient, search tolerance — to maximize site-localized glycan identification confidence.
key_facts: The optimizer tunes 6 instrument and workflow parameters.
ETD peaks at ~18% supplemental activation, while HCD peaks at ~35%.
Including SialEXO pretreatment is reported as +17% yield.
Gradient length is described as saturating above ~150 minutes.
v1.1 adds 5 reference catalogs.
